name: Weighted Hollow Hold
movementDefinition: Hold a supine hollow-body position against the added load while keeping the lower back controlled.

name: Weighted Leg Lower
movementDefinition: From supine, lower the straight legs in Weighted Leg Lower while keeping the lumbar spine controlled, then raise them again.

name: Windshield Wiper
movementDefinition: Hang from a pull-up bar, raise your legs to the ceiling. Move lower body from left to right with straight legs like a windshield wiper.

name: 3-1-1 Bodyweight Tempo Squat
movementDefinition: Stand in a comfortable squat stance with no external load. Descend for three seconds, pause at the bottom for one second, then stand in one second while keeping the knees aligned and trunk braced.

name: Altitude Landing
movementDefinition: Step from a low raised surface, land on both feet, and absorb the impact through the hips, knees, and ankles. Stabilize and hold the landing; do not add a jump.

name: Altitude Landing to Jump
movementDefinition: Step from a low raised surface, absorb the two-foot landing, and immediately rebound into a vertical jump. Land the jump softly and regain balance before resetting.

name: Banded 1.5 Squat
movementDefinition: Descend into a band-resisted squat, rise halfway, descend again, and then stand fully for one repetition.

name: Banded Accentuated Countermovement Jump
movementDefinition: Rapidly dip through the hips and knees in Banded Accentuated Countermovement Jump and immediately jump vertically using the named load or arm position.

name: Banded Ankle Dorsiflexion
movementDefinition: This exercise uses an exercise band. It targets the Soleus and Tibialis anterior.

name: Banded Ankle Plantar Flexion
movementDefinition: Banded plantarflexion is a great way to bridge the gap between plantarflexion range of motion and the more strenuous calf raises in weight bearing. This can help strengthen the calf muscles, load the Achilles tendon, and improve plantarflexion range of motion.

name: Banded Clamshell
movementDefinition: Lie on the side with the hips and knees bent and a band above the knees. Keep the feet together and pelvis stacked while lifting the top knee, then lower it under control without rolling backward.

name: Banded Glute Kickback
movementDefinition: A Banded Glute Kickback is an exercise performed on hands and knees, where you anchor a resistance band to your foot and kick your heel back and up, squeezing your glute at the top of the movement, while maintaining a stable core and flat back to target and strengthen the gluteal muscles.

name: Banded Lateral Walk
movementDefinition: Lateral walks, also known as side steps or squat walks, are a type of exercise where you move sideways in a squatting position. They can be performed with or without resistance bands. These exercises strengthen the hip abductors, glutes, and other stabilizing muscles.

name: Banded Leg Curl
movementDefinition: Standing position: Place the band under your feet in the middle of your foot and grasp it with a hammer grip.

name: Banded Single-Leg Hip Thrust
movementDefinition: Extend the hip in Banded Single-Leg Hip Thrust to raise the pelvis while keeping the ribs controlled, then lower under tension.

name: Barbell Back Squat
movementDefinition: Secure the bar across the upper back, brace the trunk, and bend the hips and knees while the knees track with the toes. Reach a controlled depth, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Barbell Box Squat
movementDefinition: Set a box behind the stance and secure any prescribed load. Sit the hips back until they contact the box under control, maintain trunk tension, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Barbell Clean
movementDefinition: Regular olympic lift clean. Pull bar from ground, catch on shoulders performing a front squat.

name: Barbell Forward Lunge
movementDefinition: Secure a barbell across the upper back and stand tall. Step one leg forward, lower until both knees are comfortably bent while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to return and alternate sides.

name: Barbell Front Squat
movementDefinition: This variation of the squat trains the hamstrings and gluteus maximus. It also works the back extensors and abductors.

name: Barbell Glute Bridge
movementDefinition: Lie supine with both feet planted and any prescribed barbell secured across the hips. Extend the hips until the trunk and thighs align, pause, then lower the pelvis under control.

name: Barbell Good Morning
movementDefinition: Stand with your feet more than shoulder-width apart (wider stance) and place your hands on your hips. Hinge at your hips and lower your upper body towards the ground while keeping your back straight then come back up.

name: Barbell Hack Squat
movementDefinition: Flex the hips and knees to descend in Barbell Hack Squat with the feet planted, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
movementDefinition: Brace the upper back on a bench with a barbell across the hips, drive through the feet to extend the hips until the torso and thighs align, then lower under control.

name: Barbell Overhead Squat
movementDefinition: The barbell is held overhead in a wide-arm snatch grip; however, it is also possible to use a closer grip if balance allows.

name: Barbell Pin Squat
movementDefinition: Set security pins to about the height of your sticking point Lower the bar, rest on the pins for 2s keeping tension Stand up with maximum force.

name: Barbell Power Clean
movementDefinition: Start with the bar over midfoot, brace, and pull it from the floor with the spine neutral. Accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, then pull under and receive the bar in a partial squat at the front rack before standing.

name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
movementDefinition: Hold the bar against the thighs with soft knees, push the hips back to lower it along the legs with a neutral spine, and extend the hips to stand.

name: Barbell Snatch
movementDefinition: Olympic lift snatch Pull barbell from ground to overhead lockout using a wide grip and overhead squat. Move bar slowly until cleared knees then explosively extend hips, pull bar up and dive under the bar Catch bar overhead with straight arms Push head forward during lockout.

name: Barbell Speed Deadlift
movementDefinition: Set up for a conventional deadlift and accelerate a submaximal bar from the floor while maintaining a braced spine, then return it under control for crisp repetitions.

name: Barbell Step-Back Lunge
movementDefinition: Hold a barbell securely across the upper back. Step one leg backward, lower until both knees are comfortably bent while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to stand and alternate sides.

name: Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlift
movementDefinition: Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlift, then extend the hips to return to standing.

name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
movementDefinition: Stand with a wide stance and the bar over midfoot, grip inside the knees, and brace the trunk. Push through the floor and extend the hips and knees to stand tall, then return the bar under control.

name: Barbell Thruster
movementDefinition: Start by doing a front squat At the top position, push the bar above your head (similar to a press) Lower the bar to the shoulders.

name: Basic Jump Rope
movementDefinition: Hold the rope handles at the sides and turn the rope primarily with the wrists. Make small two-foot jumps as it passes under the feet, land softly, and continue at a steady conditioning rhythm.

name: Belt Squat
movementDefinition: Belt Squat: a squat variation done with a belt attached to a loading machine or platform so the weight is supported at the hips instead of on the spine. It mainly trains the quads and glutes, with much less lower-back fatigue than a barbell squat. Use a full range of motion, keep the torso upright, and drive through the mid-foot.

name: Bodyweight Glute Bridge
movementDefinition: Lie on the back with both feet planted and knees bent. Brace the trunk and extend both hips until the shoulders, hips, and knees form a line, then lower the pelvis under control.

name: Bodyweight Glute Bridge Hold
movementDefinition: Lie on the back with both feet planted and extend the hips until the trunk and thighs align. Hold the bridge isometrically for the prescribed duration while keeping the pelvis level, then lower under control.

name: Bodyweight Lateral Step to Squat
movementDefinition: Stand with the feet about hip-width apart. Step laterally into a wider stance, sit the hips back into a controlled squat, stand, and bring the trailing foot in before alternating directions.

name: Bodyweight Lunge
movementDefinition: Starting position: Stand with back straight. Steps: Take a large step forward with your left leg. Bring your pelvis down until you almost touch the floor with your right knee. Bring your pelvis back up. Return to the starting position by stepping back. Repeat, switching legs each time.

name: Bodyweight Reverse Lunge
movementDefinition: Starting position: Stand straight, feet hip-width apart. Steps: Step backward with one leg so it can bend comfortably to a 90 degree angle. Slowly bend both knees to form 90 degree angles. Return to the starting position. Repeat, alternating legs.

name: Bodyweight Squat
movementDefinition: Stand with the feet about shoulder-width and no external load. Bend the hips and knees while keeping the knees aligned with the toes, reach a controlled depth, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Bodyweight Sumo Squat
movementDefinition: Stand without an external load, feet wider than shoulder width and toes turned out comfortably. Bend the hips and knees while keeping the knees aligned with the toes, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Box Jump
movementDefinition: Jump from a standing position onto the box, stretch your body, then step down again (do not jump).

name: Broomstick Hip Hinge
movementDefinition: Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Broomstick Hip Hinge, then extend the hips to return to standing.

name: Burpee
movementDefinition: Jump, lay down on your chest, do a pushup then jump, repeat.

name: Cable Pull-Through
movementDefinition: Face away from a low cable with the rope between the legs. Hinge by pushing the hips backward while keeping the spine neutral, then extend the hips to stand without pulling with the arms.

name: Clamshell
movementDefinition: Lie on one side with hips and knees bent, keep the pelvis stacked, and externally rotate the top hip by opening the knee without rolling the trunk.

name: Copenhagen Hip Adduction
movementDefinition: Lie on the side with the top leg supported on a bench and the lower leg beneath it. Lift the hips and draw the lower leg toward the bench, maintaining a straight trunk before lowering under control.

name: Cossack Squat
movementDefinition: The Cossack Squat is a multi-joint exercise that works mainly on the legs and buttocks, but also involves stabilizing muscles. It is an excellent exercise for improving strength, mobility and stability in a functional way.

name: Criss-Cross Jump
movementDefinition: Jump the feet apart, then jump them back together with one foot crossing in front of the other; alternate the leading cross while landing softly with the knees aligned.

name: Double-Kettlebell Front Squat
movementDefinition: The kettlebell front squat is a compound, multi-joint exercise that works several muscle groups.

name: Double-Leg Calf Raise
movementDefinition: The double leg heel raise is important to strengthen and create control and stability around the ankle and knee, and provide balance and control for the hip and pelvis.

name: Dragon Squat
movementDefinition: Start standing with your feet hip-width apart. Cross your right foot behind you to the left corner and back of the room while bending both knees. Return and repeat, alternating sides. Keep your hips and shoulders forward as you cross your feet and bend your knees.

name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat
movementDefinition: The Bulgarian split squat consists of performing a squat on one leg with the rear foot resting on a raised platform. Elevating your back leg on a bench creates instability and increases the range of motion of the exercise.

name: Dumbbell Countermovement Jump
movementDefinition: Stand with a light dumbbell in each hand at the sides. Dip rapidly through the hips and knees, immediately jump vertically, and land softly with the weights controlled before resetting.

name: Dumbbell Deadlift
movementDefinition: Stand with dumbbells beside the legs, hinge and flex the knees to lower them near the floor with a braced spine, then extend the hips and knees to stand.

name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge
movementDefinition: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and step one leg forward. Lower until both knees are comfortably bent while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to return and alternate sides.

name: Dumbbell Frog Pump
movementDefinition: Lie on the back with the soles of the feet together and knees opened outward, holding a dumbbell securely across the hips. Extend the hips, pause at the top, then lower the pelvis under control.

name: Dumbbell Front Squat
movementDefinition: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height and performing a squat. It targets the lower body muscles, including the quads, hamstrings, and glutes, while also engaging the core and upper body.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Reverse Lunge
movementDefinition: Step in the direction named by Dumbbell Goblet Reverse Lunge, lower through the stance hip and knee, and drive back while maintaining knee alignment.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Reverse Lunge with Knee Raise
movementDefinition: Step in the direction named by Dumbbell Goblet Reverse Lunge with Knee Raise, lower through the stance hip and knee, and drive back while maintaining knee alignment.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Split Squat
movementDefinition: Flex the hips and knees to descend in Dumbbell Goblet Split Squat with the feet planted, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
movementDefinition: Hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest. Sit the hips down between the feet while keeping the trunk braced and knees aligned with the toes, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat to Press
movementDefinition: Hold the weight at the chest, squat to depth, then stand and press it overhead in one continuous repetition.

name: Dumbbell Hang Power Clean
movementDefinition: Hold dumbbells at the sides, hinge them to about knee height, then explosively extend the hips, knees, and ankles. Pull under and receive the dumbbells at the shoulders with softly bent knees before standing.

name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
movementDefinition: This exercise involves sitting on the ground with a dumbbell resting on the hips, then thrusting the hips upward while squeezing the glutes. It is a great exercise for strengthening the glutes and improving hip mobility.

name: Dumbbell Kickstand Romanian Deadlift
movementDefinition: Stand with most of the weight on the working leg and place the other toes lightly behind for balance. Hold the dumbbells close to the legs, hinge at the hips with a braced spine, then extend the working hip to stand.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Squat
movementDefinition: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in one hand and performing a squat while stepping to the side. It targets the legs, glutes, and core muscles.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
movementDefinition: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in each hand and stepping back into a lunge position, then returning to standing. It primarily targets the glutes, hamstrings, and quadriceps.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
movementDefinition: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in each hand and bending forward at the hips while keeping the back straight, then returning to a standing position. It primarily targets the hamstrings and glutes.

name: Dumbbell Snatch
movementDefinition: Start with one dumbbell near the floor and brace the trunk. Drive through the legs and hips to accelerate it upward, guide it overhead in one motion, receive it with a stable arm and knees softly bent, then lower under control.

name: Dumbbell Split Squat
movementDefinition: Flex the hips and knees to descend in Dumbbell Split Squat with the feet planted, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Dumbbell Step-Up
movementDefinition: Load one leg through the step or split stance named by Dumbbell Step-Up, then drive through the stance foot to return.

name: Dumbbell Sumo Deadlift
movementDefinition: Lower your dumbbell to the ground between your legs. Assume a wide stance and with a straight back squat down. With the dumbbell standing upright, grip it by the top of the ‘head’ (picture 1).Keeping your chest up and core braced, push the floor away, driving back upwards to a standing position (picture 2). Repeat. If you can easily achieve 20-30 reps, use two dumbbells.

name: Dumbbell Thruster
movementDefinition: Start with the dumbbells resting on your shoulders and squat down. Push into standing and raise the dumbbells into an overhead position. Bring the dumbbells back to your shoulders and repeat.

name: Dynamic Straight-Leg Kick
movementDefinition: Stand tall and swing one straight leg forward through a comfortable range as a dynamic hamstring mobility drill. Lower it without forcing the end range and alternate legs while keeping the trunk controlled.

name: Falling Countermovement Jump
movementDefinition: Rapidly dip through the hips and knees in Falling Countermovement Jump and immediately jump vertically using the named load or arm position.

name: Fire Hydrant
movementDefinition: From quadruped, lift one bent knee laterally without rotating the pelvis, then lower it under control.

name: Hack Squat Machine Calf Raise
aliases: Calf Raise using Hack Squat Machine
movementDefinition: Place yourself on the machine with your back firmly against the backrest, the feet are on the platform for calf raises. Check that the feet are half free and that you can completely stretch the calf muscles down. With straight knees pull up your weight as much as you can. Go with a fluid movement down till the calves are completely stretched. Repeat.

name: Hack Squat Machine Leg Press
movementDefinition: Lower the hack-squat carriage by flexing the hips and knees, then press through the feet to extend both legs.

name: Hands-on-Hips Countermovement Jump
movementDefinition: Rapidly dip through the hips and knees in Hands-on-Hips Countermovement Jump and immediately jump vertically using the named load or arm position.

name: High Knees
movementDefinition: Starting positionStand straight. Steps: Run in place, putting knees as high up as is comfortable and switching legs at a quick pace.

name: High-Knee Jump
movementDefinition: Dip into a shallow squat and jump vertically, drawing both knees toward the chest near the top. Extend for a soft two-foot landing, absorb the impact, and reset before repeating.

name: High-Knee Skip
movementDefinition: Skip forward or in place, driving one knee upward as the opposite foot pushes off and the opposite arm swings. Alternate rhythmically with tall posture and soft landings.

name: Hindu Squat
movementDefinition: Start with your feet shoulder width apart and arms slightly behind your back. As you descend towards the floor, raise your heels off the ground, while keeping your back as vertical as possible. Upon attaining the bottom position, touch the hands to the heels. Then stand up ending with the heels on the ground, arms extended in front of the chest then rowing into the start position.

name: Hip Hinge
movementDefinition: Stand with softly bent knees and brace the trunk. Push the hips backward while keeping the spine neutral until tension develops in the posterior thighs, then extend the hips to return upright.

name: Hop and Hold
movementDefinition: Jump in the direction named by Hop and Hold, absorb the landing on a controlled hip and knee, and stabilize before continuing.

name: Horse Stance Hold
movementDefinition: Take a very wide stance with the toes turned slightly outward and lower into a deep squat while keeping the trunk upright. Hold the position continuously for the prescribed duration with the knees tracking over the toes, then stand under control.

name: Ice Skater
movementDefinition: Jump in the direction named by Ice Skater, absorb the landing on a controlled hip and knee, and stabilize before continuing.

name: Ice Skater to Vertical Hop
movementDefinition: Jump in the direction named by Ice Skater to Vertical Hop, absorb the landing on a controlled hip and knee, and stabilize before continuing.

name: Isometric Squat to Failure
movementDefinition: Lower into a stable squat at the prescribed depth and hold that joint position without bouncing. Maintain knee alignment and trunk tension until technical form can no longer be sustained, then stand or exit the position safely.

name: Jumping Jack
movementDefinition: A jumping jack or star jump, also called side-straddle hop in the US military, is a physical jumping exercise performed by jumping to a position with the legs spread wide and the hands going overhead, sometimes in a clap, and then returning to a position with the feet together and the arms at the sides.

name: Kettlebell Deadlift
movementDefinition: Stand over a kettlebell, hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle, and brace the trunk. Extend the hips and knees to stand tall, then hinge to return the bell between the feet.

name: Kettlebell Forward Lunge
movementDefinition: Hold a kettlebell securely at the chest or side and step one leg forward. Lower until both knees are comfortably bent while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to return and alternate sides.

name: Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift
movementDefinition: Stand wider than shoulder width with the toes turned slightly out and the kettlebell between the feet. Brace the trunk, hinge at the hips and bend the knees to grip the bell, then extend the hips and knees to stand before lowering it under control.

name: Kettlebell Swing
movementDefinition: Hike the kettlebell between the thighs from a braced hip hinge, then snap the hips forward to float it to about chest height. Let it return into the next hinge without squatting or lifting with the arms.

name: Kneeling Glute Kickback
movementDefinition: Starting position: Get down on all fours. Steps: Push one foot back until fully extended, concentrating on the gluteus muscles. Stay for one second, then return to the initial position. Repeat, alternating feet.

name: Landmine Reverse Lunge with Knee Raise
movementDefinition: Step in the direction named by Landmine Reverse Lunge with Knee Raise, lower through the stance hip and knee, and drive back while maintaining knee alignment.

name: Landmine Squat
movementDefinition: Flex the hips and knees to descend in Landmine Squat with the feet planted, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Landmine Squat to Press
movementDefinition: Hold the end of a landmine at chest height, squat while keeping the trunk braced, then stand and press the bar upward and forward. Return it to the chest before the next repetition.

name: Lateral Push-Off
movementDefinition: Push off the ground and land on one leg and regain balance before jumping to the other leg.

name: Leg Press Calf Raise
movementDefinition: Put the balls of your feet on an extended leg press pad. Use your calves to press the weight by flexing your feet/toes into a pointed position, and releasing back into a relaxed position. This exercise builds mass and strength in the Gastrocnemius and Soleus muscles as well, if not better, than any calf exercise.

name: Lying Machine Leg Curl
movementDefinition: Lay on a bench and put your calves behind the leg holder (better if they are hold on around the lower calves). Hold a grip on the bars to make sure the body is firmly in place. Bend your legs bringing the weight up, go slowly back. During the exercise the body should not move, all work is done by the legs.

name: Machine Glute Drive
movementDefinition: Set up in the glute-drive machine with the belt or pad secured across the hips and both feet planted. Extend the hips until the trunk and thighs align, pause, then lower the carriage under control.

name: Machine Glute Kickback
movementDefinition: Drive through your heel, not your toes, to hit the glute. Don’t hyperextend your lower back at the top.

name: Machine Hack Squat
movementDefinition: Set the shoulders and back against the hack-squat pads with the feet on the platform. Bend the hips and knees to a controlled depth while the knees track with the toes, then press the platform away to stand without locking abruptly.

name: Machine Hip Abduction
movementDefinition: Sit with the pelvis and back supported and the outer thighs against the pads, press both knees apart through hip abduction, and return without letting the stack drop.

name: Machine Hip Adduction
movementDefinition: Starting Position: Sit on the machine with your knees inward and against the pads. Pull the pin to release the pads. Grab the handles on the sides.b.

name: Machine Leg Extension
movementDefinition: Sit with the knee aligned to the machine pivot and the pad above the ankles. Extend the knees to raise the pad without lifting the hips, then lower it under control.

name: Machine Leg Press
movementDefinition: The leg press is a weight training exercise in which the individual pushes a weight or resistance away from them using their legs.

name: Machine Pendulum Squat
movementDefinition: Set the shoulders against the pendulum-squat pads and place both feet securely on the platform. Bend the hips and knees to a controlled depth while the knees track with the toes, then drive through the feet to return.

name: Machine Reverse Hyperextension
movementDefinition: Lie face-down on a bench with your legs hanging off the edge. Bend your hips so your thighs are vertical, bending your knees at the same time so your shins stay horizontal. Straighten out your legs so they are not bent and your toes are pointing backwards horizontally. Return to the original position, and repeat.

name: Marching High Knees
movementDefinition: March in place, lifting one knee toward hip height while the opposite arm swings forward. Keep the trunk tall, place the foot softly, and alternate at the prescribed rhythm.

name: Medicine Ball Ice Skater
movementDefinition: Jump in the direction named by Medicine Ball Ice Skater, absorb the landing on a controlled hip and knee, and stabilize before continuing.

name: Narrow-Stance Machine Leg Press
movementDefinition: Place both feet close together on the leg-press platform. Lower the carriage while the knees track with the toes, then press through the feet to extend the hips and knees without locking abruptly.

name: No-Push-Up Burpee
movementDefinition: From standing, squat and place the hands on the floor, jump or step the feet back to a high plank without performing a push-up, return the feet to the squat, then stand or jump and repeat.

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
movementDefinition: The Nordic hamstring curl is one of the best lower-body exercises to build posterior leg strength, improve knee health, and prevent injury.

name: Paused Machine Hack Squat
movementDefinition: Hack Squats but with a 1-2s pause at the bottom of the movement. This makes sure that there is no more elastic energy stored in the muscles.

name: Pistol Squat
movementDefinition: Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes pointed forward, and chest tall. Extend your leg straight out; extend both arms in front of you, at shoulder level. Brace your core and look straight ahead. Slowly squat down. (optional) pause at the bottom. Keep your (free) leg and arms extended for the whole duration.

name: Plate-Held Braced Squat
movementDefinition: Hold a weight plate straight in front of the chest with the arms extended and feet slightly wider than shoulder width. Keep the trunk braced, bend the hips and knees to squat through a controlled range, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Pogo Jump
movementDefinition: Perform Pogo Jump as quick stiff-ankle hops, rebounding immediately after each forefoot contact with minimal knee bend.

name: Prisoner Squat
movementDefinition: Stand with the feet about shoulder-width and place the hands lightly behind the head. Sit the hips down between the feet while the knees track with the toes, then drive through the feet to stand.

name: Prisoner Squat with Overhead Reach
movementDefinition: Perform a bodyweight squat, then reach both arms overhead at the top while keeping the ribs controlled.

name: Quadruped Hip Extension
movementDefinition: From quadruped, extend one hip to drive the heel backward without arching the lower back, then return.

name: Reverse Nordic Curl
movementDefinition: Natural Leg Extension is alternative to Leg Extension machine with no equipment.

name: Seated Dumbbell Calf Raise
movementDefinition: The seated dumbbell calf raise is a targeted exercise for strengthening the lower leg muscles. It follows the same movement pattern as the machine version: you place the weight on your knees, and by extending and flexing your ankles, you move the weight.

name: Seated Machine Calf Raise
movementDefinition: Sit on a bench for calf raises and check that the feet are half free and that you can completely stretch the calf muscles down. Pull your calves up, going as far (up) as you can. Make at the highest point a short pause of 1 or 2 seconds and go down.

name: Seated Machine Leg Curl
movementDefinition: From a seated leg-curl machine, flex both knees to pull the pad down and back before returning under control.

name: Shrimp Squat
movementDefinition: Lower through coordinated hip and knee flexion in Shrimp Squat, then press through the feet to stand.

name: Side-Lying Hip Abduction
movementDefinition: Lie on one side with the pelvis stacked and lift the straight top leg away from the lower leg without rolling the trunk, then lower it under control.

name: Side-Seated Single-Leg Machine Leg Press
movementDefinition: Sit sideways in the leg-press machine with one foot placed securely on the platform. Bend the working hip and knee through a controlled range, then press the platform away through the whole foot without locking the knee.

name: Single-Leg Box Squat
movementDefinition: Stand in front of a stable box on one leg with the free leg forward. Sit back under control until the hips touch the box, pause without relaxing, then drive through the stance foot to stand.

name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
movementDefinition: Stand on one leg and raise the heel through ankle plantar flexion, then lower under control through the available range.

name: Single-Leg Clock Reach
movementDefinition: Stand on one leg and reach the free foot toward successive clock positions using small controlled single-leg lunges.

name: Single-Leg Dumbbell Deadlift
movementDefinition: Hold a dumbbell and balance on the opposite or same-side stance leg as prescribed. Hinge at the stance hip while the free leg reaches back, keep the pelvis square, then extend the hip to return upright.

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
movementDefinition: A glute bridge, where you use just one leg at a time.

name: Single-Leg Hip Thrust
movementDefinition: Extend one leg while laying down and start raising your body with one leg touching the ground through the heel.

name: Single-Leg Kettlebell Deadlift
movementDefinition: This exercise involves holding a kettlebell in one hand and standing on one leg while bending forward to touch the kettlebell to the ground. It targets the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back while also improving balance and stability.

name: Single-Leg Landmine Romanian Deadlift
movementDefinition: Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Single-Leg Landmine Romanian Deadlift, then extend the hips to return to standing.

name: Single-Leg Machine Leg Extension
movementDefinition: Extend one knee against the machine pad until the leg is straight, then lower the load without moving the thigh.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
movementDefinition: Stand upright and hold weights in both hands if using loads. Brace your core and lift one leg off the ground. Keep your back straight, hinge at the hips while lowering your torso forward, ensuring you don’t rotate your hips. Lower until you feel a stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to standing position. Repeat on both sides.

name: Sled Push
movementDefinition: Brace the hands against the sled with the torso inclined and spine neutral. Drive through alternating steps to push the sled along the prescribed distance while keeping the hips and shoulders level.

name: Sliding Lateral Lunge
movementDefinition: Stand with one foot on a slider and no external load. Slide that foot outward while sitting into the opposite hip and keeping its knee aligned with the toes, then pull the sliding foot back to stand.

name: Smith Machine Split Squat
movementDefinition: Set the Smith bar across the upper back and take a split stance. Lower through both knees while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to return.

name: Smith Machine Squat
movementDefinition: Set the Smith bar across the upper back and place the feet in a stable stance. Bend the hips and knees to a controlled depth while keeping the knees aligned, then press through the feet to stand.

name: Squat Jump
movementDefinition: Descend into a shallow squat, then rapidly extend the hips, knees, and ankles to jump vertically. Land softly with the knees aligned, absorb the impact, and reset before the next repetition.

name: Squat Thrust
movementDefinition: From standing, squat and place both hands on the floor, jump or step the feet back to a high plank, then return them to the squat and stand. Repeat continuously at the prescribed conditioning pace.

name: Standing Cable Hip Abduction
movementDefinition: Attach a low cable to an ankle cuff and stand side-on while holding a stable support. Move the working leg laterally away from the body without leaning the trunk, then return under control and repeat on both sides.

name: Standing Cable Hip Adduction
movementDefinition: Keep your hips square and still, pulling the cable across your opposing leg using only your inner thigh. Don’t let your torso lean or twist — no side bending to help the rep.

name: Standing Machine Calf Raise
movementDefinition: Get onto the calf raises machine, you should able to completely push your calves down. Stand straight, don't make a hollow back and don't bend your legs. Pull yourself up as high as you can. Make a small pause of 1 - 2 seconds and go slowly down.

name: Standing Machine Leg Curl
movementDefinition: Stand in the machine and flex one knee to draw the heel toward the glute while keeping the thigh fixed.

name: Step Jack
movementDefinition: Stand tall, step one foot out while sweeping both arms overhead, then return the foot and arms to the start. Alternate sides continuously at a controlled conditioning pace.

name: Supine Hip Abduction
movementDefinition: Lie supine with both legs extended. Keep the pelvis still as one leg moves laterally away from the midline, return it under control, and complete the prescribed work on both sides.

name: Tibialis Raise
movementDefinition: Stand up straight Lift the balls of your feet, resting on your heels.

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
movementDefinition: Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Trap Bar Deadlift, then extend the hips to return to standing.

name: Trap Bar Squat
movementDefinition: A combination of squat and deadlift principles with the use of a hex/trap bar, easing stress on the lower back by better centering the weight.

name: Walking Barbell Lunge
movementDefinition: Hold a barbell on the upper back and alternate forward lunges, stepping through into each repetition.

name: Walking Dumbbell Lunge
movementDefinition: Take two dumbbells in your hands, stand straight, feet about shoulder wide. Take one long step so that the front knee is approximately forming a right angle. The back leg is streched, the knee is low but doesn't touch the ground. "Complete" the step by standing up and repeat the movement with the other leg.

name: Wall Ball
movementDefinition: Get a medicine ball, shoulder width stance, squat, thrust the ball as high as possible against the wall and catch.

name: Wall Sit
movementDefinition: Place the back against a wall and slide down until the hips and knees reach a comfortable squat angle. Keep both feet planted and hold the position for the prescribed duration without resting on the thighs.

name: Wide-Stance Low Box Squat
movementDefinition: Unrack the bar and set your stance wide, beyond your hips. Push your hips back and sit down to a box that takes you below parallel. Sit completely down, do not touch and go. Then explosively stand up. Stay tight in your upper back and torso throughout the movement.

name: Wide-Stance Machine Leg Press
movementDefinition: Sit on the machine and put your feet on the platform, a bit more than shoulder wide. The feet are turned outwards by a few degrees. Lower the weight so much, that the knees form a right angle. Push immediately the platform up again, without any pause. When in the lower position, the knees point a bit outwards and the movement should be always fluid.

name: Band Face Pull
movementDefinition: Face pulls with band – standing horizontal pull. Grab the band at face height, pull elbows high and wide towards your face while squeezing shoulder blades together. Trains upper back, rear shoulders and improves posture.

name: Band Pull-Apart with External Rotation
movementDefinition: Hold a band at shoulder height, pull the hands apart by retracting the shoulder blades, and finish with the forearms rotating outward. Reverse the motion under control without shrugging.

name: Banded External Rotation
movementDefinition: Hold a resistance band with the elbows bent to 90 degrees and fixed beside the torso, rotate the forearms outward without arching or turning the trunk, and return slowly.

name: Barbell Clean and Jerk
movementDefinition: Olympic lift clean and jerk. Combination of clean and jerk in one motion.

name: Barbell Clean and Press
movementDefinition: Start with a barbell on the floor, clean it to the front rack by extending the hips and turning the elbows under, then press it overhead before lowering under control.

name: Barbell High Pull
movementDefinition: Use a light barbell, perform explosive lift up starting from underneath knee cap level. Lift/raise explosively using hips, at shoulder level. Tempo: 2111.

name: Barbell Jerk
movementDefinition: Olympic lift jerk Hold bar in front on shoulders like push press Bend and extend knees pushing bar up while diving under the bar Catch bar overhead with straight arms using lunge Move feet parallel while holding bar in lockout.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
movementDefinition: Setup: Stand with feet together and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Position: Rest the bar on your upper chest. Press: Brace your core and push the bar straight up until your arms lock out. Finish: Control the bar back down to your chest.

name: Barbell Pin Overhead Press
movementDefinition: Set security pins to the height of your lower chin Rest bar on pins keeping tension Raise bar over head.

name: Barbell Push Press
movementDefinition: Hold a barbell in the front rack, brace, and make a short vertical dip through the knees and hips. Drive the floor away and use that momentum to press the bar overhead, then return it to the rack under control.

name: Behind-the-Back Cable Lateral Raise
movementDefinition: Stand side-on to a low cable with the handle in the outside hand and the cable passing behind the body. Lead with the elbow to raise the arm laterally to shoulder height, then lower under control without leaning or shrugging.

name: Bent-Over Dumbbell Face Pull
movementDefinition: This exercise involves using dumbbells to perform a bent over face pull, which targets the upper back and shoulders. The movement involves pulling the weights towards the face while keeping the elbows high and squeezing the shoulder blades together.

name: Bent-Over Dumbbell High Pull
movementDefinition: Bend over slightly while holding two dumbbells. Pull the dumbbells up to your chest, keeping your elbows as high as you can.

name: Cable External Rotation
movementDefinition: Stand side-on to a cable set near elbow height, keep the working elbow fixed at 90 degrees beside the torso, and rotate the forearm outward before returning slowly.

name: Cable Face Pull
movementDefinition: Set a rope cable near face height and pull it toward the face with the elbows high. Retract the shoulder blades and rotate the forearms back, then return under control without shrugging.

name: Cable Front Raise
movementDefinition: Raise the handle in a slight arc with controlled movement. Don’t swing your torso to start the rep.

name: Cable Internal Rotation
movementDefinition: Stand side-on to a cable with the working elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked against the torso. Rotate the forearm inward across the abdomen without turning the trunk, then return under control.

name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
movementDefinition: Stand between crossed cables and hold the opposite handles at shoulder height. Open the arms in a reverse-fly path while retracting the shoulder blades, then return under control.

name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
movementDefinition: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand and elbows softly bent. Raise the arms out to the sides in a reverse-fly path, squeeze the shoulder blades gently, then lower under control.

name: Codman Pendulum
movementDefinition: Support the torso with the free arm, let the working arm hang relaxed, and create small circles by gently shifting the body rather than actively lifting the shoulder.

name: Cross-Cable Upright Row
movementDefinition: Stand between low pulleys and hold the crossed cable handles in front of the thighs. Lead with the elbows to draw the handles upward along the torso, then lower under control without shrugging.

name: Double-Kettlebell Clean and Press
movementDefinition: Full-body muscles building exercise. This exercise provides a huge range of benefits in terms of strength & size and is extremely functional.

name: Dumbbell Arnold Press
movementDefinition: Press the resistance overhead from shoulder height in Dumbbell Arnold Press while keeping the trunk braced, then lower it.

name: Dumbbell Bradford Press
movementDefinition: From a front hold of the DBs in a OHP, press above the head, bring back towards the rear of the shoulders, down, and press back forward.

name: Dumbbell Circle Press
movementDefinition: Sit against a moderately inclined bench with dumbbells overhead. Lower them beside the shoulders, rotate the palms upward as the arms sweep forward to meet in front of the chest, then reverse the circular path back overhead.

name: Dumbbell Devil's Press
movementDefinition: Hold two dumbbells, perform a controlled burpee with the hands on the handles, then jump or step the feet forward and drive both dumbbells overhead in one fluid swing. Lower them safely before the next repetition.

name: Dumbbell Diagonal Shoulder Press
movementDefinition: Stand or sit holding dumbbells near the shoulders. Press them upward and slightly outward along a comfortable diagonal until the elbows extend, then lower them along the same path without arching the lower back.

name: Dumbbell Front Raise
movementDefinition: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and elbows softly bent. Raise the arms forward to about shoulder height without leaning back, then lower the weights under control.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
movementDefinition: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and elbows softly bent. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height without shrugging or leaning, then lower under control.

name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Row
movementDefinition: Hold a dumbbell in each hand. Bend at your hips to make your back almost parallel to the floor. Let your arms hang down. Keep your arms in line with your shoulders. bend and lift your elbows out to the sides until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Slowly return and repeat.

name: Dumbbell Scaption
movementDefinition: Starting position: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Keep a slight bend in your elbows, shoulders relaxed and shoulder blades pulled back. Execution: Slowly raise the dumbbells forward and slightly to the side, at about a 30–45° angle from your body (between a front raise and a lateral raise).

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
movementDefinition: Sit on a bench, the back rest should be almost vertical. Take two dumbbells and bring them up to shoulder height, the palms and the elbows point during the whole exercise to the front. Press the weights up, at the highest point they come very near but don't touch. Go slowly down and repeat.

name: Dumbbell Turkish Get-Up
movementDefinition: Starting on back, move to the standing position with dumbbell in one hand. Switch hands between reps.

name: Dumbbell Upright Row
movementDefinition: Hold a dumbbell in each hand in front of your body. Keep your palms towards your body. Lift you hands straight up until your hands are under your chin, then lower them. Repeat the exercise.

name: EZ-Bar Upright Row
movementDefinition: Stand straight, your feet are shoulder-width apart. Hold the SZ-bar with an overhand grip on your thighs, the arms are stretched. Lift the bar close to the body till your chin. The elbows point out so that at the highest point they form a V. Make here a short pause before going slowly down and repeating the movement.

name: Front-of-Body Cable Lateral Raise
movementDefinition: Stand side-on to a low cable with its line of pull passing in front of the body. Lead with the elbow to raise the working arm laterally to shoulder height, then lower under control without shrugging.

name: Half-Kneeling Dumbbell Press
movementDefinition: Press the resistance overhead from shoulder height in Half-Kneeling Dumbbell Press while keeping the trunk braced, then lower it.

name: Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
movementDefinition: From a half-kneeling stance, press the landmine bar upward and forward with one arm without rotating or extending the trunk.

name: Handstand Push-Up
movementDefinition: Hold a handstand against a wall or with secure support. Bend the elbows to lower the head through a controlled range, then press back to straight arms while maintaining trunk tension.

name: Hindu Push-Up
movementDefinition: Begin with the hips high, sweep the chest forward and low between the hands, then extend the arms as the chest rises. Reverse or lift the hips back to the start while keeping the movement controlled.

name: Incline Dumbbell Overhead Press
movementDefinition: Sit on a bench with 45° incline Ellbows 45° out Move dumbbells down until upper arms are parallel to shoulders Move dumbbells up, meeting overhead.

name: Incline Dumbbell Y-Raise
movementDefinition: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with light dumbbells. Raise the straight or softly bent arms forward and outward into a Y without shrugging, then lower under control.

name: Landmine Press
movementDefinition: Press the free end of a landmine bar upward and forward from shoulder height while keeping the trunk braced.

name: Machine Lateral Raise
movementDefinition: Sit in the lateral-raise machine with the upper arms against the pads. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height without shrugging, then lower the pads under control.

name: Machine Reverse Fly
movementDefinition: Sit facing the reverse-fly machine and sweep the arms outward through horizontal abduction while keeping the chest supported.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
movementDefinition: Press the machine handles overhead from shoulder height, then lower until the elbows return below the wrists.

name: Pike Push-Up
movementDefinition: Push Up performed from a pike position (optional to have feet elevated).

name: Plate Bus Driver
movementDefinition: Sitting with a Weight Plate, used as wheel, in both hands; raised slightly below eye level.

name: Plate Front Raise
movementDefinition: Stand holding a weight plate at its sides with the elbows softly bent. Raise the plate forward to about shoulder height without leaning back, then lower it under control.

name: Prone Banded Press
movementDefinition: Lie prone, maintain band tension, and press the arms overhead without shrugging or extending the lower back.

name: Pseudo Planche Push-Up
movementDefinition: You should have the shoulder line in front of your wrists an perform a push-up, maintaining the shoulder in the same position.

name: Seated Dumbbell Lateral Raise
movementDefinition: Sit with a slight forward lean and raise the dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height with softly bent elbows, then lower without using momentum.

name: Seated Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
movementDefinition: Seated, bent 45 deg forward. Arms fully stretched out, raise arms up to shoulder height and back down.

name: Side-Lying Dumbbell External Rotation
movementDefinition: Lie on the side with the working arm on top, elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked against the torso. Rotate the forearm upward while the upper arm stays still, then lower the dumbbell under control.

name: Side-Lying Dumbbell Internal Rotation
movementDefinition: Lie on the working side with that elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked against the torso. Rotate the forearm toward the abdomen against the dumbbell, then return through a controlled range without moving the upper arm.

name: Single-Arm Cable Lateral Raise
aliases: Lateral Rows on Cable, One Armed
movementDefinition: Stand side-on to a low cable and hold one handle with the working arm. Raise the arm laterally to about shoulder height without leaning or shrugging, then lower under control.

name: Single-Arm Cable Rear Delt Fly
movementDefinition: Cable rear delt fly (single arm): Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle set around shoulder height. With a slight bend in the elbow, pull the handle out and back in a wide arc so the upper arm moves away from the body. Keep your torso still and focus on the rear delt doing the work. Use light to moderate weight and controlled reps.

name: Single-Arm Cross-Body Cable Pulldown
movementDefinition: Stand angled to a high cable with one arm extended. Keeping the elbow nearly straight, sweep the handle diagonally down and across the body toward the opposite hip, then return under control without rotating the torso.

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Shoulder Press
movementDefinition: Press the dumbbell vertically overhead in a controlled motion until your arm is fully extended, without locking the elbow. Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position at shoulder height.

name: Single-Arm Perpendicular Landmine Row
movementDefinition: Using a landmine attachment with the barbell running at 90 degrees to either side, hinge slightly at the hips and grip the barbell by the plate sleeve. Raise the barbell to chest height, with your upper arm parallel to the floor at the top of the movement.

name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
movementDefinition: Press the Smith-machine bar overhead from shoulder height while keeping the trunk braced, then lower it along the guide rails.

name: Smith Machine Upright Row
movementDefinition: Stand at the guided-bar machine with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Pull the bar upward along the torso with the elbows leading to a comfortable height, then lower under control.

name: Standing Rotational Landmine Press
movementDefinition: Stand at the end of a landmine, press the bar forward and upward with one arm while allowing controlled trunk rotation, then return to the starting position.

name: Straight-Bar Cable Front Raise
aliases: Cable Front Raise with a small bar
movementDefinition: Face away from a low pulley with an overhand grip on the straight bar, raise it forward to shoulder height with nearly straight elbows, and lower without swinging.

name: Tuck Planche
movementDefinition: Place both hands on the floor or parallettes, lean the shoulders forward beyond the wrists, and draw the knees tightly toward the chest. Lift both feet and hold the tucked body supported on straight arms without resting the knees on the elbows.
